newsSince: Bullish but largely confirmatory since June 9: Unimicron targets a record 2026 on AI ABF + system-board demand (Jun 1 Taiwan News / DigiTimes), raised 2026 capex to a record NT$34B, with AI now ~60% of ABF revenue; BofA upgraded to Buy and Bernstein names it a top Taiwan AI pick. ABF tight-supply is now guided to persist through 2028.

sources.label: Unimicron targets record 2026 on AI / ABF push

verdicts.rankRationale: On the residual-alpha-vs-passives lens the hidden/under-owned premise keeps eroding: +345% YTD, BofA Buy upgrade and Bernstein top-pick status make it consensus, and it trades ~2x MS's bull-case NT$500 target. Strong ABF tailwind is real but fully recognized; crowding pushes it down on this lens.
verdicts.bull: ABF sold out through 2028; record 2026 target; AI ~60% of ABF revenue.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but now consensus; sell-side targets already exceeded.
verdicts.bear: +345% YTD trades ~2x bull-case target; zero residual hidden alpha; flow-driven.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: ABF tight supply through 2028 / capacity ramp

verdicts.rankRationale: On the packaging/substrate-scarcity master list the bottleneck thesis is intact and modestly strengthening: ABF sold-out guidance extended through 2028, record capex, AI mix rising to ~60%. That is genuine choke-point reinforcement (vs pure price), warranting a small + while rank holds given already-high recognition.
verdicts.bull: Central ABF/advanced-substrate scarcity node; demand visibility to 2028; record capex backs supply.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck strong but rerating has priced much of it; execution/pricing to watch.
verdicts.bear: Substrate scarcity could ease faster than guided; +345% YTD leaves little margin for error.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: ABF/HBM-attach substrate scarcity and capacity ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: Substrate oversupply or AI capex air-pocket
